context: You can already say what you do. Now say how often - and your Spanish gains a personality. Always, sometimes, never: these tiny words turn a single fact into a habit.
explanation: Three frequency words do most of the work: siempre (always), a veces (sometimes) and normalmente (usually). They sit right before the verb - siempre trabajo, a veces como tarde - or at the very start of the sentence. You already know nunca (never) and a menudo (often), so you can mix all five.

examples.en: I always work in the morning
I usually have breakfast at seven

examples.note: los + a day = every week on that day: los domingos = on Sundays.

examples.es: como pescado una vez a la semana
examples.en: I eat fish once a week
examples.note: una vez a la semana = once a week.
